title: Curaleaf Reports Third Quarter 2025 Results: Solid Growth and Robust Cash Generation
text: Curaleaf Holdings reported third-quarter 2025 financial results with net revenue of $320.2 million, a year-over-year decrease of 3% compared to Q3 2024 net revenue of $330.5 million. However, sequentially, net revenue increased 2% compared to Q2 2025 net revenue of $314.5 million. The international segment continued its strong trajectory, delivering 12% sequential growth and 56% year-over-year growth. In contrast, the domestic segment remained stable and achieved modest growth, despite price compression continuing to be a headwind. Adjusted gross margin improved to 50%, an increase of 115 basis points both sequentially and versus the prior year. Net loss attributable to Curaleaf Holdings, Inc. from continuing operations was $54.5 million or net loss per share from continuing operations of $0.07, with adjusted net loss from continuing operations of $48.2 million or adjusted net loss per share from continuing operations of $0.06. CEO Boris Jordan noted that their Return to Our Roots plan initiated 12 months ago is focused on enhancing product quality, driving growth, expanding margins, and optimizing cash flow.
